highlights.title: Anthropic Is Making Its Claude Chatbot More Appealing to Consumers
highlights.description: Artificial intelligence startup Anthropic PBC, which originally aimed its Claude chatbot at businesses, is looking to capitalize on recent inroads with consumers by making the software more appealing to everyday people.

highlights.title: Datacenter to become Arm’s biggest business ‘soon’
highlights.description: Someone other than Meta is buying $1bn of its new AGI chips

highlights.title: Amazon Cuts Workers, Phases Out Grocery Service in Singapore
highlights.description: Amazon.com Inc. said a “small number of roles” in Singapore will be impacted as it phases out local fulfillment including Amazon Fresh in the city-state.

highlights.title: SoftBank shares surge over 18% as Japan tech-fueled rally lifts Nikkei 225 to record highs
highlights.description: The benchmark Nikkei 225 surged over 5% to a record high as Japan reopened after extended Golden Week holidays.

highlights.title: EU Startup Fund Makes First Bet With $160 Million Round in UK’s Quantum Motion
highlights.description: Kembara, a technology growth fund backed by the European Union, made its first investment, supporting a British startup that builds quantum computers running on silicon chips.

highlights.title: Humanoid Robots to Drive Next Leg of China Export Dominance
highlights.description: China’s early lead in humanoid robots will help power the next phase of its global manufacturing and export dominance, according to new research from Morgan Stanley.

highlights.title: Andreessen Horowitz in Funding Round for Swedish AI Startup Pit
highlights.description: Andreessen Horowitz has led a $16 million funding round for Pit, an artificial intelligence startup based in Sweden.

highlights.title: Korea Surpasses Canada as World’s Seventh-Largest Stock Market
highlights.description: South Korea’s equity market has overtaken Canada’s as the world’s seventh largest, propelled by insatiable demand for chips powering artificial intelligence.

highlights.title: Samsung says its Galaxy Watch can predict fainting with 'high accuracy'
highlights.description: The feature would allow users to get into a safe position or call for help.

highlights.title: Top Trump Aide Says Administration Won’t Pick Winners in AI Race
highlights.description: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles said the US government would refrain from choosing winners and losers in artificial intelligence, the latest signal from a top aide to President Donald Trump as his administration prepares new AI policy directives.

ai.title: Top Trump Aide Says Administration Won’t Pick Winners in AI Race
ai.description: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles said the US government would refrain from choosing winners and losers in artificial intelligence, the latest signal from a top aide to President Donald Trump as his administration prepares new AI policy directives.

ai.title: Five architects of the AI economy explain where the wheels are coming off
ai.description: Earlier this week, five people who touch every layer of the AI supply chain sat down at the Milken Global Conference in Beverly Hills, where they talked with TechCrunch about everything from chip shortages to orbital data centers to the possibility that the whole architecture that undergirds the tech is wrong.

ai.title: Chipmaker Silex Microsystems Shares Soar in Stockholm Debut
ai.description: Silex Microsystems AB surged in its first day of trading on Nasdaq Stockholm after an initial public offering that raised about 2 billion Swedish kronor ($220 million) for the specialist microchip-maker and its Chinese shareholder Sai MicroElectronics Inc.

ai.title: Alibaba Shares Outpace Tencent’s as Chip Exposure Fuels Demand
ai.description: The torrid stock rally in Asian chipmakers is driving a divergence between China’s two internet giants, with Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. gaining an edge over rival Tencent Holdings Ltd. due to investor enthusiasm about its ambitious semiconductor unit.

chips_cloud.title: Datacenter to become Arm’s biggest business ‘soon’
chips_cloud.description: Someone other than Meta is buying $1bn of its new AGI chips

ev_autonomous.title: EU Startup Fund Makes First Bet With $160 Million Round in UK’s Quantum Motion
ev_autonomous.description: Kembara, a technology growth fund backed by the European Union, made its first investment, supporting a British startup that builds quantum computers running on silicon chips.

ev_autonomous.title: EVs Next for Hong Kong Firm's Water-Soluble Battery Binder
ev_autonomous.description:
A water-soluble battery binder developed by Hong Kong-based GRST enables battery recycling with less pollution and greater recovery of critical metals, the company's chief strategy officer, Frank Harley, said in an interview at the BloombergNEF Summit New York on April 22, 2026. 

The firm "is making the first stage of recycling a lot simpler," Harley said.  Hong Kong-based GRST is "working with seven equipment manufacturers in China and the end customers for them are EVs, typically European EVs and consumer electronics," he said. (Source: Bloomberg)

robotics.title: Humanoid Robots to Drive Next Leg of China Export Dominance
robotics.description: China’s early lead in humanoid robots will help power the next phase of its global manufacturing and export dominance, according to new research from Morgan Stanley.

regulation.title: Minister gives Palantir's NHS platform a clean bill of health
regulation.description: £330M contract defended as value for money despite concerns over IP and lock-in

business.title: GIC, BlackRock Funding Makes Skyroot India’s First Space Unicorn
business.description: Skyroot Aerospace Pvt. has become India’s first space-tech unicorn after securing $60 million in fresh funding from GIC Pte and Sherpalo Ventures, lifting its valuation to $1.1 billion.

business.title: Kimi Chatbot Maker Moonshot AI Valued at $20 Billion in Meituan-Led Round
business.description: Moonshot AI has raised about $2 billion in its latest funding round, signaling growing investor appetite for Chinese startups rivaling Silicon Valley’s leaders.
